iteration_summary.iter_004: Schema enforcement → auto-fixer fixes YAML problems
iteration_summary.iter_005: Type graph extraction → completeness gap identified
iteration_summary.iter_007: Self-healing → 4/6 compile, healing works but imprecise
iteration_summary.iter_008: Multi-version voting → 6/6 on simple, 0/6 on complex modules
iteration_summary.iter_009: Two-stage interface-first → Stage 1 for PrimUtils started
iteration_summary.iter_010: Hybrid deterministic type extraction → 4/6 compile OK

key_findings: Multi-version voting: 100% simple modules, 0% complex — LLM non-determinism cannot overcome BSV domain gaps
Self-healing: works for simple type errors but structural errors are unfixable via hint changes
Both approaches hit same ceiling: LLMs lack deep BSV scoping/syntax knowledge for complex modules
Simple modules (typedefs, structs, functions) reliably generatable from YAML
Complex modules (rules, methods, FSMs) need either deterministic extraction or reference-guided generation
YAML serialization (save_yaml) breaks format — needs robust quoting
The hybrid approach (iter_006 design) remains the most promising unscaled direction
Deterministic type extraction: 4/6 modules compile (100% type correctness). Hybrid approach eliminates LLM BSV domain knowledge ceiling for types.
Remaining 2 failures (Arbitration, WorkCompGen) are dependency chain gaps — need Utils, DataTypes, Controller packages; NOT type extraction issues.
BSV interface type definitions vs member declarations require careful distinction (leading space in regex, keyword counting after paren removal).
next_direction: Implement iter_006 hybrid: deterministic type extraction from original BSV + LLM behavioral generation. Or: reference-diff generation using original BSV as structural template.
